steps.title: How bracket tournaments work
steps.content: In each round, remaining players are paired into matches. Each match produces exactly one loser who is eliminated and one winner who advances to the next round. Therefore, each round eliminates exactly half of the remaining players.

steps.title: Calculate eliminations in round 1
steps.content:
Starting with 240 players:
  • Number of matches: 240 ÷ 2 = 120
  • Losers eliminated: 120
We can express this as: $$p = 240 \cdot \frac{1}{2}$$

steps.title: Generalize the pattern to any round r
steps.content:
Since each round eliminates half the remaining players, round 2 would eliminate from 120 survivors: $240 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 = 60$.

Round 3: $240 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^3 = 30$. The exponent increases by 1 each round, giving the formula that matches choice C — C is correct.
